bullets.content: IF user provides an email address -> call find_user_id_by_email(email) THEN call get_user_details(user_id)
IF user provides first name, last name, and zip code -> call find_user_id_by_name_zip(first_name, last_name, zip) THEN call get_user_details(user_id)
IF the user identifies an order (gives an order ID) OR before performing any order-specific action -> call get_order_details(order_id)

IF the user requests a product variant change or asks about product options (availability, material, compatibility, size, etc.) -> call get_product_details(product_id) to retrieve available variants and prices
IF multiple orders exist or the user is unsure which order contains the item -> ask for the order ID or offer to look through the user's orders (then call get_order_details for the selected order)

IF identity is verified AND order.status == 'delivered' AND user requests an exchange -> verify target variant availability with get_product_details(product_id) THEN call exchange_delivered_order_items(order_id, item_ids, new_item_ids, payment_method_id)
IF identity is verified AND order.status == 'delivered' AND user requests a return -> call return_delivered_order_items(order_id, item_ids, payment_method_id)
IF identity is verified AND order.status == 'pending' AND user requests cancellation -> call cancel_pending_order(order_id, reason)

IF identity is verified AND order.status == 'pending' AND user requests an item modification (size/variant) -> verify target variant availability with get_product_details(product_id) THEN call modify_pending_order_items(order_id, item_ids, new_item_ids, payment_method_id)

IF performing an exchange or modification that changes price -> compute price_difference = new_price - old_price and include payment_method_id (the order's payment method) in the tool call; if price_difference < 0, issue refund to that payment method; if price_difference > 0, charge that payment method

IF executing an action via a tool call (exchange/return/modify/cancel) -> ensure the tool call includes order_id, affected item_ids, the chosen new_item_ids (for exchange/modify), and payment_method_id (the order's payment method)

IF an exchange/return/modify/cancel tool call completes successfully -> rely on the tool result to set order status appropriately (examples observed: 'exchange requested', 'return requested', 'pending (item modified)', 'cancelled')
IF multiple candidate orders/items or ambiguity exists -> ask the user to provide the specific order ID before proceeding
IF presenting product variant options -> list available variants with options and prices and explicitly ask the user which variant they prefer
IF an action will change the order (exchange, return request, cancellation, pending-item modification) -> ask the user to explicitly confirm before proceeding (e.g., request 'yes' confirmation)

IF a refund will be issued -> inform the user of the refund amount, the destination payment method (including identifying info such as card brand and last four digits or PayPal), and the expected timeframe (example used: within 5-7 business days)
IF an action is completed -> provide a concise summary of what changed, the new order status, and notify the user that an email with return/next-steps instructions will be sent when applicable

IF the user requests a refund to a different payment method than the original -> inform the user that refunds can only go to the original payment method or to an existing gift card on the account and offer to proceed with those options or escalate to a human agent
IF the user has not provided email OR (first name, last name, zip) -> do not perform account-specific actions until identity info is provided and get_user_details has been called
IF order.status == 'delivered' -> allowed actions: exchanges and returns; disallowed: pending-item modifications and pending-order cancellations (use delivered-specific tools)
IF order.status == 'pending' -> allowed actions: item modifications or cancellation (use modify_pending_order_items or cancel_pending_order); exchanges/returns for delivered items are not applicable
IF a requested target variant is unavailable -> do not perform the exchange or modification to that variant; offer only available alternatives (after checking get_product_details)

IF user requests refund to a different payment method than the order's original payment method AND there is no existing gift card on account -> refuse the redirect and offer refund only to the original payment method or to an existing gift card

IF a pending-order item has been modified via modify_pending_order_items -> subsequent item modifications on that same order are not permitted (i.e., disallow further modify_pending_order_items calls for that order after modification)

deltas.content: [acti-008] IF identity is verified AND order.status == 'pending' AND user requests to update the shipping address for that pending order -> call modify_pending_order_address(order_id, address1, address2, city, state, country, zip). Ensure the tool call includes order_id and the full new address. Require explicit user confirmation before applying the change (see conf-003).
deltas.rationale: Trajectories show agents updating the shipping address for pending orders via modify_pending_order_address, but the playbook lacked a rule for this action.

deltas.content: [acti-009] IF identity is verified AND the user requests to update their account default address -> call modify_user_address(user_id, address1, address2, city, state, country, zip). Ensure get_user_details has been called and include the user_id and full new address in the tool call. Confirm the change with the user.
deltas.rationale: Agents updated a user's default account address via modify_user_address in the trajectories; the playbook does not include an account-address modification rule.

deltas.content: [prec-003] IF order.status == 'pending' -> allowed actions: item modifications, shipping-address updates, or cancellation (use modify_pending_order_items, modify_pending_order_address, or cancel_pending_order); exchanges/returns for delivered items are not applicable.
deltas.rationale: Observed flows included updating the shipping address on pending orders; precondition rules should explicitly allow address updates for pending orders and reference the appropriate tool.

deltas.content: IF a user requests multiple different action types (returns, exchanges, or pending-item modifications) for items within the same order -> do not perform mixed action types in a single operation on that order; ask the user to choose one action type per order and proceed only after explicit confirmation.
deltas.rationale: Conversation 1 shows the agent refusing to do a return and an exchange on items from the same delivered order in one operation; the playbook lacked an explicit rule forbidding mixed action types on the same order.

deltas.content: IF identity is verified AND order.status == 'pending' AND the user requests a shipping address change -> call modify_pending_order_address(order_id, address1, address2, city, state, country, zip) (confirm address with the user before applying). Address modifications are allowed for pending orders and are tracked separately from item-level modifications.
deltas.rationale: Trajectories (tasks 112 and 104) include modify_pending_order_address tool calls to update shipping for pending orders; the playbook did not include an explicit action rule for address changes.

deltas.content: IF executing an action via a tool call (exchange/return/modify/cancel/address-modify) -> ensure the tool call includes order_id and the relevant fields required for that action: for item-level actions include affected item_ids, the chosen new_item_ids (for exchange/modify), and payment_method_id (the order's payment method) as applicable; for address modifications include the full new address fields (address1, address2, city, state, country, zip).
deltas.rationale: Existing acti-006 required item_ids/new_item_ids/payment_method_id for all action tool calls, but address-modify calls (observed in tasks 112 and 104) require address fields instead of item_ids/new_item_ids.

deltas.content: IF a refund will be issued -> inform the user of the refund amount, the destination payment method (including identifying info such as card brand and last four digits, PayPal, or gift card id and/or remaining balance when refunding to a gift card), and the expected timeframe (example used: within 5-7 business days).
deltas.rationale: Trajectories include refunds to gift cards (task 112) as well as cards/PayPal (tasks 13, 104); confirmation guidance should explicitly cover gift-card refunds and provide identifying info for them.

deltas.content: IF performing an exchange or modification that changes price -> compute price_difference = new_price - old_price and include payment_method_id (the chosen payment method from the user's account) in the tool call. By default use the order's original payment method; if the user explicitly requests a different payment method that is already on their account, use that method but obtain explicit user confirmation before proceeding. If price_difference < 0, issue refund to the chosen payment method; if price_difference > 0, charge the chosen payment method. Do not send refunds to payment methods not on the user's account.
deltas.rationale: Agents in the trajectories honored the customer's explicit choice to use a different payment method on the same account for exchanges/refunds rather than always using the original order payment method.

deltas.content: IF the user requests a refund to a different payment method than the original -> inform the user that refunds default to the original payment method but can be issued to another payment method on their account or to an existing gift card if they explicitly request and confirm; refunds cannot be sent to external payment methods not on the account. If no eligible on-account method or gift card exists, offer refund only to the original payment method, offer a gift card if available, or offer escalation to a human agent.
deltas.rationale: Trajectories show agents allowed refunds/charges to a different card on the customer's account after explicit confirmation; update messaging to reflect this allowed path while prohibiting off-account refunds.

deltas.content: IF user requests refund to a different payment method than the order's original AND the requested method is NOT on the user's account AND there is no existing gift card on account -> refuse the redirect and offer refund only to the original payment method or to an existing gift card. IF the requested method is on the user's account -> allow the redirect but require explicit user confirmation before proceeding.
deltas.rationale: Existing rule disallowed redirects unless a gift card existed; trajectories show redirects to other on-account payment methods were accepted after user confirmation, so the precondition should be refined to permit on-account redirects with confirmation while still blocking off-account redirects.

deltas.content: IF an agent cannot fulfill a user's request due to system limitations (inventory, payment constraints), policy restrictions, or upon explicit user request for human help -> call transfer_to_human_agents(summary) with a concise summary of the customer's issue and offer to transfer. Record that transfer was requested/completed and notify the user that they are being connected to a human agent.
deltas.rationale: Trajectories include an instance where the agent escalated/ transferred the customer to a human agent when unable to complete an exchange due to payment constraints; the playbook should define escalation behavior and the transfer_to_human_agents tool usage.

deltas.rationale: Trajectories show address changes on pending orders using modify_pending_order_address (conversations 3 and 5); the playbook has no rule for handling address updates.

deltas.content: IF the user asks to reuse the shipping address from a previous order or to use an address saved on a prior order -> call get_order_details(previous_order_id) to retrieve that order's shipping address, present the retrieved address to the user, and ask for confirmation before applying it to the current order.
deltas.rationale: Agents retrieved and reused an address from a prior order when the user requested it (conversation 3); the playbook lacks an explicit rule for this flow.

deltas.content: IF a user requests a faster-than-standard refund or processing timeframe (e.g., asks to guarantee processing within fewer days than policy) -> inform the user of the standard timeframe, state that the agent cannot guarantee a faster processing time, and offer to escalate the request to a human agent who may be able to assist with expediting.
deltas.rationale: A user requested a 3-day refund window and the agent could not guarantee it but offered to escalate to a human agent (conversation 1); the playbook should explicitly capture this standard response.

deltas.content: IF identity is verified AND order.status == 'pending' AND user requests cancellation -> ask the user for a cancellation reason (provide common options if helpful), ask the user to explicitly confirm the cancellation, THEN call cancel_pending_order(order_id, reason). After completion, inform the user of the refund amount, the refund destination payment method (including identifying info), and the expected timeframe.
deltas.rationale: Conversations show agents collecting a cancellation reason and requesting explicit confirmation before calling cancel_pending_order, and then summarizing refunds (conversation 4 and 5). acti-003 should require collecting a reason and explicit confirmation to match observed behavior.

## Information Gathering
  [info-001] IF user provides an email address -> call find_user_id_by_email(email) THEN call get_user_details(user_id)
  [info-002] IF user provides first name, last name, and zip code -> call find_user_id_by_name_zip(first_name, last_name, zip) THEN call get_user_details(user_id)
  [info-003] IF the user identifies an order (gives an order ID) OR before performing any order-specific action -> call get_order_details(order_id)
  [info-004] IF the user requests a product variant change or asks about product options (availability, material, compatibility, size, etc.) -> call get_product_details(product_id) to retrieve available variants and prices
  [info-005] IF multiple orders exist or the user is unsure which order contains the item -> ask for the order ID or offer to look through the user's orders (then call get_order_details for the selected order)

## Action Execution
  [acti-001] IF identity is verified AND order.status == 'delivered' AND user requests an exchange -> verify target variant availability with get_product_details(product_id) THEN call exchange_delivered_order_items(order_id, item_ids, new_item_ids, payment_method_id)
  [acti-002] IF identity is verified AND order.status == 'delivered' AND user requests a return -> call return_delivered_order_items(order_id, item_ids, payment_method_id)
  [acti-003] IF identity is verified AND order.status == 'pending' AND user requests cancellation -> call cancel_pending_order(order_id, reason)
  [acti-004] IF identity is verified AND order.status == 'pending' AND user requests an item modification (size/variant) -> verify target variant availability with get_product_details(product_id) THEN call modify_pending_order_items(order_id, item_ids, new_item_ids, payment_method_id)
  [acti-005] IF performing an exchange or modification that changes price -> compute price_difference = new_price - old_price and include payment_method_id (the order's payment method) in the tool call; if price_difference < 0, issue refund to that payment method; if price_difference > 0, charge that payment method
  [acti-006] IF executing an action via a tool call (exchange/return/modify/cancel) -> ensure the tool call includes order_id, affected item_ids, the chosen new_item_ids (for exchange/modify), and payment_method_id (the order's payment method)
  [acti-007] IF an exchange/return/modify/cancel tool call completes successfully -> rely on the tool result to set order status appropriately (examples observed: 'exchange requested', 'return requested', 'pending (item modified)', 'cancelled')

## Confirmation Communication
  [conf-001] IF multiple candidate orders/items or ambiguity exists -> ask the user to provide the specific order ID before proceeding
  [conf-002] IF presenting product variant options -> list available variants with options and prices and explicitly ask the user which variant they prefer
  [conf-003] IF an action will change the order (exchange, return request, cancellation, pending-item modification) -> ask the user to explicitly confirm before proceeding (e.g., request 'yes' confirmation)
  [conf-004] IF a refund will be issued -> inform the user of the refund amount, the destination payment method (including identifying info such as card brand and last four digits or PayPal), and the expected timeframe (example used: within 5-7 business days)
  [conf-005] IF an action is completed -> provide a concise summary of what changed, the new order status, and notify the user that an email with return/next-steps instructions will be sent when applicable
  [conf-006] IF the user requests a refund to a different payment method than the original -> inform the user that refunds can only go to the original payment method or to an existing gift card on the account and offer to proceed with those options or escalate to a human agent

## Precondition Rules
  [prec-001] IF the user has not provided email OR (first name, last name, zip) -> do not perform account-specific actions until identity info is provided and get_user_details has been called
  [prec-002] IF order.status == 'delivered' -> allowed actions: exchanges and returns; disallowed: pending-item modifications and pending-order cancellations (use delivered-specific tools)
  [prec-003] IF order.status == 'pending' -> allowed actions: item modifications or cancellation (use modify_pending_order_items or cancel_pending_order); exchanges/returns for delivered items are not applicable
  [prec-004] IF a requested target variant is unavailable -> do not perform the exchange or modification to that variant; offer only available alternatives (after checking get_product_details)
  [prec-005] IF user requests refund to a different payment method than the order's original payment method AND there is no existing gift card on account -> refuse the redirect and offer refund only to the original payment method or to an existing gift card
  [prec-006] IF a pending-order item has been modified via modify_pending_order_items -> subsequent item modifications on that same order are not permitted (i.e., disallow further modify_pending_order_items calls for that order after modification)

deltas.content: IF user provides a user_id -> call get_user_details(user_id) to retrieve account information.
deltas.rationale: Trajectories show interactions where a user supplied an account user_id; the system should accept that and call get_user_details directly.

deltas.content: IF the user has not provided any of the following: email OR (first name, last name, zip) OR user_id -> do not perform account-specific actions until identity info is provided and get_user_details has been called.
deltas.rationale: Clarifies that providing a user_id is an acceptable identity signal; avoid blocking account-specific actions when a valid user identifier is supplied.

deltas.content: IF identity is verified AND order.status == 'pending' AND user requests an address update for that order -> call modify_pending_order_address(order_id, address fields) to update the order address, THEN call modify_user_address(user_id, address fields) to update the account address (if the user requests), and return updated order/user details.
deltas.rationale: One trajectory required updating both the pending order address and the account address; the playbook had no explicit rule for address updates.

deltas.content: IF performing an exchange or modification that changes price -> compute price_difference = new_price - old_price. Ask the user which payment method they want to use for any additional charge (if price_difference > 0). Include payment_method_id (the chosen payment method) in the tool call for charging the difference. If price_difference < 0, issue the refund to the original order payment method (or to an existing gift card on the account if the user prefers and one exists). Clearly inform the user of the refund amount, the destination payment method, and the expected timeframe.
deltas.rationale: Trajectories show customers choosing a different payment method to pay extra (e.g., PayPal or gift card) while refunds consistently go to the original method or an account gift card; the rule should allow a user-chosen payment method for charges and enforce refund destination rules.

deltas.content: IF executing an action via a tool call (exchange/return/modify/cancel) -> ensure the tool call includes order_id, affected item_ids, the chosen new_item_ids (for exchange/modify), and payment_method_id (the payment method chosen for any additional charge). For refunds (price_difference < 0 or return), ensure the refund destination is the original order payment method or an existing gift card per refund policy.
deltas.rationale: Tool calls in the trajectories included payment_method_id chosen by the user when paying price differences; the rule should require inclusion of the chosen payment method for charges and explicitly enforce refund destination constraints.

Playbook needs a rule preventing automatic propagation and forbidding changes to processed orders.

deltas.content: IF a user requests an account/default address update -> explicitly ask whether they want that address applied to any existing orders, and if so, ask which specific order IDs to update. If the user does not explicitly request order updates, assume existing orders remain unchanged.
deltas.rationale: Trajectories show the assistant asking and confirming whether to update both the account and a pending order (conversation 22). The playbook should require explicit confirmation to avoid unintended changes to order shipping addresses.

deltas.rationale: In Conversation 4 the user demanded a refund to an Amex that wasn't on file and asked to be escalated; the assistant escalated to a human agent. The playbook should explicitly allow escalation when the user insists but policy prevents the requested refund routing.

deltas.rationale: Tool calls in the trajectories used an account gift card as the payment_method_id for pending-order modifications; the playbook must permit using gift cards when requested.

deltas.content: IF order.status == 'cancelled' -> the assistant cannot reinstate or 'undo' the cancellation. Offer to place a new order on the user's behalf or transfer to a human agent for possible reinstatement or exceptions.
deltas.rationale: A trajectory demonstrated that reinstating a cancelled order is outside the assistant's capabilities and the user was transferred to a human agent. This should be an explicit precondition the assistant checks and communicates.

## Action Execution
  [acti-001] IF identity is verified AND order.status == 'delivered' AND user requests an exchange -> verify target variant availability with get_product_details(product_id) THEN call exchange_delivered_order_items(order_id, item_ids, new_item_ids, payment_method_id)
  [acti-002] IF identity is verified AND order.status == 'delivered' AND user requests a return -> call return_delivered_order_items(order_id, item_ids, payment_method_id)
  [acti-003] IF identity is verified AND order.status == 'pending' AND user requests cancellation -> call cancel_pending_order(order_id, reason)
  [acti-004] IF identity is verified AND order.status == 'pending' AND user requests an item modification (size/variant) -> verify target variant availability with get_product_details(product_id) THEN call modify_pending_order_items(order_id, item_ids, new_item_ids, payment_method_id)
  [acti-005] IF performing an exchange or modification that changes price -> compute price_difference = new_price - old_price and include payment_method_id (the order's payment method) in the tool call; if price_difference < 0, issue refund to that payment method; if price_difference > 0, charge that payment method
  [acti-006] IF executing an action via a tool call (exchange/return/modify/cancel) -> ensure the tool call includes order_id, affected item_ids, the chosen new_item_ids (for exchange/modify), and payment_method_id (the order's payment method)
  [acti-007] IF an exchange/return/modify/cancel tool call completes successfully -> rely on the tool result to set order status appropriately (examples observed: 'exchange requested', 'return requested', 'pending (item modified)', 'cancelled')

## Confirmation Communication
  [conf-001] IF multiple candidate orders/items or ambiguity exists -> ask the user to provide the specific order ID before proceeding
  [conf-002] IF presenting product variant options -> list available variants with options and prices and explicitly ask the user which variant they prefer
  [conf-003] IF an action will change the order (exchange, return request, cancellation, pending-item modification) -> ask the user to explicitly confirm before proceeding (e.g., request 'yes' confirmation)
  [conf-004] IF a refund will be issued -> inform the user of the refund amount, the destination payment method (including identifying info such as card brand and last four digits or PayPal), and the expected timeframe (example used: within 5-7 business days)
  [conf-005] IF an action is completed -> provide a concise summary of what changed, the new order status, and notify the user that an email with return/next-steps instructions will be sent when applicable
  [conf-006] IF the user requests a refund to a different payment method than the original -> inform the user that refunds can only go to the original payment method or to an existing gift card on the account and offer to proceed with those options or escalate to a human agent

## Precondition Rules
  [prec-001] IF the user has not provided email OR (first name, last name, zip) -> do not perform account-specific actions until identity info is provided and get_user_details has been called
  [prec-002] IF order.status == 'delivered' -> allowed actions: exchanges and returns; disallowed: pending-item modifications and pending-order cancellations (use delivered-specific tools)
  [prec-003] IF order.status == 'pending' -> allowed actions: item modifications or cancellation (use modify_pending_order_items or cancel_pending_order); exchanges/returns for delivered items are not applicable
  [prec-004] IF a requested target variant is unavailable -> do not perform the exchange or modification to that variant; offer only available alternatives (after checking get_product_details)
  [prec-005] IF user requests refund to a different payment method than the order's original payment method AND there is no existing gift card on account -> refuse the redirect and offer refund only to the original payment method or to an existing gift card
  [prec-006] IF a pending-order item has been modified via modify_pending_order_items -> subsequent item modifications on that same order are not permitted (i.e., disallow further modify_pending_order_items calls for that order after modification)

Exchanges/returns for delivered items are not applicable.
deltas.rationale: Trajectories show users requesting cancellation of single items from a pending order but the system only supported cancelling entire orders (conversation 57). Clarify pending-order cancellation applies to whole orders to avoid ambiguity.

deltas.content: IF multiple exchanges or modifications across more than one order will result in both charges and refunds -> compute and present a per-order breakdown (charge or refund amounts, payment method used for each), show the net total to be charged or refunded, and request explicit user confirmation before proceeding.
deltas.rationale: When a user requested exchanges across two delivered orders that produced both a charge and a refund, the assistant computed and presented a net total and asked for confirmation (conversation 95). The playbook should require this behavior so users clearly understand multi-order financial effects.

deltas.content: IF the user asks the agent to locate which order(s) contain specific items (user can't provide an order ID) -> offer to search the user's orders, then iterate through the user's orders calling get_order_details(order_id) to find matching items and report which order(s) contain them before taking any action.
deltas.rationale: Users sometimes ask the agent to find items across their orders rather than provide an order ID. In multiple trajectories the assistant searched the user's orders via successive get_order_details calls to locate items (conversations 81 and 107). Capture this as an explicit information-gathering rule.

bullets.content: IF user provides an email address -> call find_user_id_by_email(email) THEN call get_user_details(user_id)
IF user provides first name, last name, and zip code -> call find_user_id_by_name_zip(first_name, last_name, zip) THEN call get_user_details(user_id)
IF the user identifies an order (gives an order ID) OR before performing any order-specific action -> call get_order_details(order_id)

IF the user requests a product variant change or asks about product options (availability, material, compatibility, size, etc.) -> IF a product_id is provided, call get_product_details(product_id) to retrieve available variants and prices; OTHERWISE call list_all_product_types() to identify the relevant product_id(s), then call get_product_details(product_id) for the selected product(s) to retrieve variant availability and prices.
IF multiple orders exist or the user is unsure which order contains the item -> ask for the order ID or offer to look through the user's orders (then call get_order_details for the selected order)

IF identity is verified AND order.status == 'delivered' AND user requests an exchange -> verify target variant availability with get_product_details(product_id) THEN call exchange_delivered_order_items(order_id, item_ids, new_item_ids, payment_method_id)
IF identity is verified AND order.status == 'delivered' AND user requests a return -> call return_delivered_order_items(order_id, item_ids, payment_method_id)

IF identity is verified AND order.status == 'pending' AND user requests cancellation -> call cancel_pending_order(order_id, reason). Note: cancel_pending_order cancels the entire pending order (not individual items). If the user wants to change or replace specific items in a pending order, use modify_pending_order_items(order_id, item_ids, new_item_ids, payment_method_id) instead. Before performing a cancellation that affects the order, ask the user to explicitly confirm the cancellation.

IF identity is verified AND order.status == 'pending' AND user requests an item modification (size/variant) -> verify target variant availability with get_product_details(product_id) THEN call modify_pending_order_items(order_id, item_ids, new_item_ids, payment_method_id)

IF performing an exchange or modification that changes price -> compute price_difference = new_price - old_price and include a payment_method_id in the tool call; by default use the order's original payment method, but if the user explicitly requests an alternate valid payment method on their account (for example PayPal or a gift card) and that method can be used for charges, use that method for price increases. For price decreases (refunds), refund must go to the original payment method or to an existing gift card on the account. Ensure the chosen payment_method_id is included in the tool call.

IF executing an action via a tool call (exchange/return/modify/cancel) -> ensure the tool call includes order_id, affected item_ids, the chosen new_item_ids (for exchange/modify), and payment_method_id. The payment_method_id should normally be the order's original payment method, but may be an existing gift card on the account if the customer requests that payment method and it has sufficient balance.

IF an exchange/return/modify/cancel tool call completes successfully -> rely on the tool result to set order status appropriately (examples observed: 'exchange requested', 'return requested', 'pending (item modified)', 'cancelled')
IF multiple candidate orders/items or ambiguity exists -> ask the user to provide the specific order ID before proceeding
IF presenting product variant options -> list available variants with options and prices and explicitly ask the user which variant they prefer
IF an action will change the order (exchange, return request, cancellation, pending-item modification) -> ask the user to explicitly confirm before proceeding (e.g., request 'yes' confirmation)

IF a refund will be issued -> inform the user of the refund amount, the destination payment method (including identifying info such as card brand and last four digits, PayPal, or gift card id and/or remaining balance when refunding to a gift card), and the expected timeframe (example used: within 5-7 business days).
IF an action is completed -> provide a concise summary of what changed, the new order status, and notify the user that an email with return/next-steps instructions will be sent when applicable

IF the user requests a refund to a different payment method than the original -> inform the user that refunds default to the original payment method but can be issued to another payment method on their account or to an existing gift card if they explicitly request and confirm; refunds cannot be sent to external payment methods not on the account. If no eligible on-account method or gift card exists, offer refund only to the original payment method, offer a gift card if available, or offer escalation to a human agent.

IF the user has not provided any of the following: email OR (first name, last name, zip) OR user_id -> do not perform account-specific actions until identity info is provided and get_user_details has been called.

IF order.status == 'delivered' -> allowed actions: exchanges and returns via delivered-specific tools; disallowed: modifying items using pending-order modification tools and cancelling via pending-order cancellation (use delivered-specific workflows instead).

IF order.status == 'pending' -> allowed actions: modify items or cancel the entire order using modify_pending_order_items or cancel_pending_order; cancellations apply to the whole order only (individual-item cancellations are not supported); exchanges/returns for delivered-item workflows are not applicable.
IF a requested target variant is unavailable -> do not perform the exchange or modification to that variant; offer only available alternatives (after checking get_product_details)

IF user requests refund to a different payment method than the order's original AND the requested method is NOT on the user's account AND there is no existing gift card on account -> refuse the redirect and offer refund only to the original payment method or to an existing gift card. IF the requested method is on the user's account -> allow the redirect but require explicit user confirmation before proceeding.

IF a pending-order item has been modified via modify_pending_order_items -> subsequent item modifications on that same order are not permitted (i.e., disallow further modify_pending_order_items calls for that order after modification)

IF identity is verified AND order.status == 'pending' AND the user requests a shipping/address change for that order -> confirm the full new address with the user and obtain explicit confirmation before applying the change (conf-003); once confirmed, call modify_pending_order_address(order_id, address1, address2, city, state, country, zip) ensuring the tool call includes the order_id and the full new address; after the update, confirm the updated shipping address with the user, note any effects on shipping or total price (if applicable), return the updated order details, ensure address modifications are only performed for pending orders and are tracked separately from item-level modifications, and perform the address update before any pending-order item modifications or cancellations.

IF identity is verified AND the user requests to update their account/default/profile address -> ensure get_user_details has been called to obtain the user_id, then call modify_user_address(user_id, address1, address2, city, state, country, zip) including the full new address; after the call return the updated user details and confirm the change with the user.

IF a user requests multiple different action types (returns, exchanges, or pending-item modifications) for items within the same order -> do not perform mixed action types in a single operation on that order; ask the user to choose one action type per order and proceed only after explicit confirmation.

IF an agent cannot fulfill a user's request due to system limitations (inventory, payment constraints), policy restrictions, or upon explicit user request for human help -> call transfer_to_human_agents(summary) with a concise summary of the customer's issue and offer to transfer. Record that transfer was requested/completed and notify the user that they are being connected to a human agent.

IF the user asks to reuse the shipping address from a previous order or to use an address saved on a prior order -> call get_order_details(previous_order_id) to retrieve that order's shipping address, present the retrieved address to the user, and ask for confirmation before applying it to the current order.

IF a user requests expedited processing or a faster-than-standard refund/processing timeframe (e.g., asks to guarantee processing within fewer days than policy) -> inform the user of the standard timeframe, state that you cannot guarantee a faster processing time, and offer to escalate the request to a human agent or support team who may be able to assist with expediting.
IF user provides a user_id -> call get_user_details(user_id) to retrieve account information.

IF identity is verified AND the user requests an order-address change or to set their account default to an address from an existing order: if order.status == 'pending' and the user requests an address update for that order, call modify_pending_order_address(order_id, address fields) to update the order address; if the request uses an address from an existing order, call get_order_details(order_id) to retrieve the address; require the user to explicitly confirm any change to their account/default address, and only then call modify_user_address(user_id, address1, address2, city, state, country, zip) to update the account address; return the updated order and/or user details.

IF the user updates their account/default address -> do NOT automatically change existing orders' shipping addresses. Orders must be updated explicitly. Additionally, IF order.status == 'processed' (or otherwise already fulfilled) -> do not allow shipping address changes for that order; inform the user the address cannot be changed for processed/fulfilled orders and offer alternatives (e.g., contact support or update future orders).

IF a user requests an account/default address update -> explicitly ask whether they want that address applied to any existing orders, and if so, ask which specific order IDs to update. If the user does not explicitly request order updates, assume existing orders remain unchanged.

IF the user requests a refund to a different payment method than the order's original AND there is no existing gift card on account AND the user insists on an alternative refund destination or requests escalation -> offer to transfer the conversation to a human agent and perform transfer_to_human_agents(summary) upon the user's confirmation. Do not process refunds to alternative payment methods without prior authorization or an available gift card.

IF a user requests cancellation of an individual item within a pending order -> do not cancel individual items; inform the user that cancellations apply to the entire order and offer alternatives (cancel the entire order or, if the item is already delivered, initiate a return for that item).

IF performing returns, exchanges, or modifications that involve items from more than one order -> execute a separate tool call for each affected order (each call must include that order_id, its affected item_ids, the chosen new_item_ids where applicable, and that order's payment_method_id).

IF multiple actions will result in refunds or charges across several orders -> provide a consolidated summary listing each action, the refund/charge amount per action, the destination payment method for each amount (including identifying info where available), and the total net refund/charge across all actions. Ask the user to explicitly confirm before proceeding.

[acti-008] IF order.status == 'pending' AND the user requests removal of an individual item from the order (i.e., delete the item) -> do not remove single items from the order; instead offer to cancel the entire order with cancel_pending_order(order_id, reason) or, if the user wants a different variant/item in its place, offer modify_pending_order_items (after verifying product availability with get_product_details).

[info-006] IF a user requests an aggregated summary across past orders (e.g., total spent on a product/category or list of all purchased variants) -> verify identity with get_user_details(user_id) (after find_user_id_by_email or find_user_id_by_name_zip as needed), then call get_order_details for the user's relevant orders and compute the requested aggregate (sum, counts, list of items). Present the total and a supporting breakdown (orders and item entries) to the user.

IF the user requests escalation to a human agent OR the user's request is outside the assistant's permitted scope (examples: reinstating a cancelled order, forcing a refund to a different payment method when no gift card exists), THEN call transfer_to_human_agents(summary) to transfer the conversation and include a concise summary of the user's identity, relevant order IDs, and the customer's requested action.

IF order.status == 'cancelled' -> the assistant cannot reinstate or 'undo' the cancellation. Offer to place a new order on the user's behalf or transfer to a human agent for possible reinstatement or exceptions.

IF the user asks about products available (e.g., 'cheapest X', product catalog, or product options) -> call list_all_product_types() if needed to resolve the product_id for the named product type, THEN call get_product_details(product_id) to retrieve variants; compute the cheapest available variant (select variant where available == true with minimum price) and present its item_id, price, and option details to the user.

IF a user requests to change their default/profile address -> ask whether they would like that new default address applied to any pending orders and explicitly confirm before proceeding to update pending orders.

IF order.status == 'return requested' -> disallow processing additional automated return requests for that same order via standard return tool calls; instead, offer to escalate to a human agent to handle any further return requests or special handling.

IF multiple exchanges or modifications across more than one order will result in both charges and refunds -> compute and present a per-order breakdown (charge or refund amounts, payment method used for each), show the net total to be charged or refunded, and request explicit user confirmation before proceeding.

IF the user asks the agent to locate which order(s) contain specific items (user can't provide an order ID) -> offer to search the user's orders, then iterate through the user's orders calling get_order_details(order_id) to find matching items and report which order(s) contain them before taking any action.

  [info-008] [info-006] IF a user requests an aggregated summary across past orders (e.g., total spent on a product/category or list of all purchased variants) -> verify identity with get_user_details(user_id) (after find_user_id_by_email or find_user_id_by_name_zip as needed), then call get_order_details for the user's relevant orders and compute the requested aggregate (sum, counts, list of items). Present the total and a supporting breakdown (orders and item entries) to the user.
  [info-009] IF the user asks about products available (e.g., 'cheapest X', product catalog, or product options) -> call list_all_product_types() if needed to resolve the product_id for the named product type, THEN call get_product_details(product_id) to retrieve variants; compute the cheapest available variant (select variant where available == true with minimum price) and present its item_id, price, and option details to the user.
  [info-010] IF the user asks the agent to locate which order(s) contain specific items (user can't provide an order ID) -> offer to search the user's orders, then iterate through the user's orders calling get_order_details(order_id) to find matching items and report which order(s) contain them before taking any action.

## Action Execution
  [acti-001] IF identity is verified AND order.status == 'delivered' AND user requests an exchange -> verify target variant availability with get_product_details(product_id) THEN call exchange_delivered_order_items(order_id, item_ids, new_item_ids, payment_method_id)
  [acti-002] IF identity is verified AND order.status == 'delivered' AND user requests a return -> call return_delivered_order_items(order_id, item_ids, payment_method_id)
  [acti-003] IF identity is verified AND order.status == 'pending' AND user requests cancellation -> call cancel_pending_order(order_id, reason). Note: cancel_pending_order cancels the entire pending order (not individual items). If the user wants to change or replace specific items in a pending order, use modify_pending_order_items(order_id, item_ids, new_item_ids, payment_method_id) instead. Before performing a cancellation that affects the order, ask the user to explicitly confirm the cancellation.
  [acti-004] IF identity is verified AND order.status == 'pending' AND user requests an item modification (size/variant) -> verify target variant availability with get_product_details(product_id) THEN call modify_pending_order_items(order_id, item_ids, new_item_ids, payment_method_id)
  [acti-005] IF performing an exchange or modification that changes price -> compute price_difference = new_price - old_price and include a payment_method_id in the tool call; by default use the order's original payment method, but if the user explicitly requests an alternate valid payment method on their account (for example PayPal or a gift card) and that method can be used for charges, use that method for price increases. For price decreases (refunds), refund must go to the original payment method or to an existing gift card on the account. Ensure the chosen payment_method_id is included in the tool call.
  [acti-006] IF executing an action via a tool call (exchange/return/modify/cancel) -> ensure the tool call includes order_id, affected item_ids, the chosen new_item_ids (for exchange/modify), and payment_method_id. The payment_method_id should normally be the order's original payment method, but may be an existing gift card on the account if the customer requests that payment method and it has sufficient balance.
  [acti-007] IF an exchange/return/modify/cancel tool call completes successfully -> rely on the tool result to set order status appropriately (examples observed: 'exchange requested', 'return requested', 'pending (item modified)', 'cancelled')
  [acti-008] IF identity is verified AND order.status == 'pending' AND the user requests a shipping/address change for that order -> confirm the full new address with the user and obtain explicit confirmation before applying the change (conf-003); once confirmed, call modify_pending_order_address(order_id, address1, address2, city, state, country, zip) ensuring the tool call includes the order_id and the full new address; after the update, confirm the updated shipping address with the user, note any effects on shipping or total price (if applicable), return the updated order details, ensure address modifications are only performed for pending orders and are tracked separately from item-level modifications, and perform the address update before any pending-order item modifications or cancellations.
  [acti-009] IF identity is verified AND the user requests to update their account/default/profile address -> ensure get_user_details has been called to obtain the user_id, then call modify_user_address(user_id, address1, address2, city, state, country, zip) including the full new address; after the call return the updated user details and confirm the change with the user.
  [acti-011] IF an agent cannot fulfill a user's request due to system limitations (inventory, payment constraints), policy restrictions, or upon explicit user request for human help -> call transfer_to_human_agents(summary) with a concise summary of the customer's issue and offer to transfer. Record that transfer was requested/completed and notify the user that they are being connected to a human agent.
  [acti-013] IF identity is verified AND the user requests an order-address change or to set their account default to an address from an existing order: if order.status == 'pending' and the user requests an address update for that order, call modify_pending_order_address(order_id, address fields) to update the order address; if the request uses an address from an existing order, call get_order_details(order_id) to retrieve the address; require the user to explicitly confirm any change to their account/default address, and only then call modify_user_address(user_id, address1, address2, city, state, country, zip) to update the account address; return the updated order and/or user details.
  [acti-017] IF performing returns, exchanges, or modifications that involve items from more than one order -> execute a separate tool call for each affected order (each call must include that order_id, its affected item_ids, the chosen new_item_ids where applicable, and that order's payment_method_id).
  [acti-018] [acti-008] IF order.status == 'pending' AND the user requests removal of an individual item from the order (i.e., delete the item) -> do not remove single items from the order; instead offer to cancel the entire order with cancel_pending_order(order_id, reason) or, if the user wants a different variant/item in its place, offer modify_pending_order_items (after verifying product availability with get_product_details).
  [acti-019] IF the user requests escalation to a human agent OR the user's request is outside the assistant's permitted scope (examples: reinstating a cancelled order, forcing a refund to a different payment method when no gift card exists), THEN call transfer_to_human_agents(summary) to transfer the conversation and include a concise summary of the user's identity, relevant order IDs, and the customer's requested action.

## Confirmation Communication
  [conf-001] IF multiple candidate orders/items or ambiguity exists -> ask the user to provide the specific order ID before proceeding
  [conf-002] IF presenting product variant options -> list available variants with options and prices and explicitly ask the user which variant they prefer
  [conf-003] IF an action will change the order (exchange, return request, cancellation, pending-item modification) -> ask the user to explicitly confirm before proceeding (e.g., request 'yes' confirmation)
  [conf-004] IF a refund will be issued -> inform the user of the refund amount, the destination payment method (including identifying info such as card brand and last four digits, PayPal, or gift card id and/or remaining balance when refunding to a gift card), and the expected timeframe (example used: within 5-7 business days).
  [conf-005] IF an action is completed -> provide a concise summary of what changed, the new order status, and notify the user that an email with return/next-steps instructions will be sent when applicable
  [conf-006] IF the user requests a refund to a different payment method than the original -> inform the user that refunds default to the original payment method but can be issued to another payment method on their account or to an existing gift card if they explicitly request and confirm; refunds cannot be sent to external payment methods not on the account. If no eligible on-account method or gift card exists, offer refund only to the original payment method, offer a gift card if available, or offer escalation to a human agent.
  [conf-007] IF a user requests expedited processing or a faster-than-standard refund/processing timeframe (e.g., asks to guarantee processing within fewer days than policy) -> inform the user of the standard timeframe, state that you cannot guarantee a faster processing time, and offer to escalate the request to a human agent or support team who may be able to assist with expediting.
  [conf-009] IF a user requests an account/default address update -> explicitly ask whether they want that address applied to any existing orders, and if so, ask which specific order IDs to update. If the user does not explicitly request order updates, assume existing orders remain unchanged.
  [conf-010] IF multiple actions will result in refunds or charges across several orders -> provide a consolidated summary listing each action, the refund/charge amount per action, the destination payment method for each amount (including identifying info where available), and the total net refund/charge across all actions. Ask the user to explicitly confirm before proceeding.
  [conf-011] IF a user requests to change their default/profile address -> ask whether they would like that new default address applied to any pending orders and explicitly confirm before proceeding to update pending orders.
  [conf-012] IF multiple exchanges or modifications across more than one order will result in both charges and refunds -> compute and present a per-order breakdown (charge or refund amounts, payment method used for each), show the net total to be charged or refunded, and request explicit user confirmation before proceeding.

## Precondition Rules
  [prec-001] IF the user has not provided any of the following: email OR (first name, last name, zip) OR user_id -> do not perform account-specific actions until identity info is provided and get_user_details has been called.
  [prec-002] IF order.status == 'delivered' -> allowed actions: exchanges and returns via delivered-specific tools; disallowed: modifying items using pending-order modification tools and cancelling via pending-order cancellation (use delivered-specific workflows instead).

IF order.status == 'pending' -> allowed actions: modify items or cancel the entire order using modify_pending_order_items or cancel_pending_order; cancellations apply to the whole order only (individual-item cancellations are not supported); exchanges/returns for delivered-item workflows are not applicable.
  [prec-004] IF a requested target variant is unavailable -> do not perform the exchange or modification to that variant; offer only available alternatives (after checking get_product_details)
  [prec-005] IF user requests refund to a different payment method than the order's original AND the requested method is NOT on the user's account AND there is no existing gift card on account -> refuse the redirect and offer refund only to the original payment method or to an existing gift card. IF the requested method is on the user's account -> allow the redirect but require explicit user confirmation before proceeding.
  [prec-006] IF a pending-order item has been modified via modify_pending_order_items -> subsequent item modifications on that same order are not permitted (i.e., disallow further modify_pending_order_items calls for that order after modification)
  [prec-007] IF a user requests multiple different action types (returns, exchanges, or pending-item modifications) for items within the same order -> do not perform mixed action types in a single operation on that order; ask the user to choose one action type per order and proceed only after explicit confirmation.
  [prec-008] IF the user updates their account/default address -> do NOT automatically change existing orders' shipping addresses. Orders must be updated explicitly. Additionally, IF order.status == 'processed' (or otherwise already fulfilled) -> do not allow shipping address changes for that order; inform the user the address cannot be changed for processed/fulfilled orders and offer alternatives (e.g., contact support or update future orders).
  [prec-009] IF the user requests a refund to a different payment method than the order's original AND there is no existing gift card on account AND the user insists on an alternative refund destination or requests escalation -> offer to transfer the conversation to a human agent and perform transfer_to_human_agents(summary) upon the user's confirmation. Do not process refunds to alternative payment methods without prior authorization or an available gift card.
  [prec-010] IF a user requests cancellation of an individual item within a pending order -> do not cancel individual items; inform the user that cancellations apply to the entire order and offer alternatives (cancel the entire order or, if the item is already delivered, initiate a return for that item).
  [prec-011] IF order.status == 'cancelled' -> the assistant cannot reinstate or 'undo' the cancellation. Offer to place a new order on the user's behalf or transfer to a human agent for possible reinstatement or exceptions.
  [prec-012] IF order.status == 'return requested' -> disallow processing additional automated return requests for that same order via standard return tool calls; instead, offer to escalate to a human agent to handle any further return requests or special handling.

all_deltas.content: [acti-008] IF identity is verified AND order.status == 'pending' AND user requests to update the shipping address for that pending order -> call modify_pending_order_address(order_id, address1, address2, city, state, country, zip). Ensure the tool call includes order_id and the full new address. Require explicit user confirmation before applying the change (see conf-003).
all_deltas.rationale: Trajectories show agents updating the shipping address for pending orders via modify_pending_order_address, but the playbook lacked a rule for this action.

all_deltas.content: [acti-009] IF identity is verified AND the user requests to update their account default address -> call modify_user_address(user_id, address1, address2, city, state, country, zip). Ensure get_user_details has been called and include the user_id and full new address in the tool call. Confirm the change with the user.
all_deltas.rationale: Agents updated a user's default account address via modify_user_address in the trajectories; the playbook does not include an account-address modification rule.

all_deltas.content: [prec-003] IF order.status == 'pending' -> allowed actions: item modifications, shipping-address updates, or cancellation (use modify_pending_order_items, modify_pending_order_address, or cancel_pending_order); exchanges/returns for delivered items are not applicable.
all_deltas.rationale: Observed flows included updating the shipping address on pending orders; precondition rules should explicitly allow address updates for pending orders and reference the appropriate tool.

all_deltas.content: IF a user requests multiple different action types (returns, exchanges, or pending-item modifications) for items within the same order -> do not perform mixed action types in a single operation on that order; ask the user to choose one action type per order and proceed only after explicit confirmation.
all_deltas.rationale: Conversation 1 shows the agent refusing to do a return and an exchange on items from the same delivered order in one operation; the playbook lacked an explicit rule forbidding mixed action types on the same order.

all_deltas.content: IF identity is verified AND order.status == 'pending' AND the user requests a shipping address change -> call modify_pending_order_address(order_id, address1, address2, city, state, country, zip) (confirm address with the user before applying). Address modifications are allowed for pending orders and are tracked separately from item-level modifications.
all_deltas.rationale: Trajectories (tasks 112 and 104) include modify_pending_order_address tool calls to update shipping for pending orders; the playbook did not include an explicit action rule for address changes.

all_deltas.content: IF executing an action via a tool call (exchange/return/modify/cancel/address-modify) -> ensure the tool call includes order_id and the relevant fields required for that action: for item-level actions include affected item_ids, the chosen new_item_ids (for exchange/modify), and payment_method_id (the order's payment method) as applicable; for address modifications include the full new address fields (address1, address2, city, state, country, zip).
all_deltas.rationale: Existing acti-006 required item_ids/new_item_ids/payment_method_id for all action tool calls, but address-modify calls (observed in tasks 112 and 104) require address fields instead of item_ids/new_item_ids.

all_deltas.content: IF a refund will be issued -> inform the user of the refund amount, the destination payment method (including identifying info such as card brand and last four digits, PayPal, or gift card id and/or remaining balance when refunding to a gift card), and the expected timeframe (example used: within 5-7 business days).
all_deltas.rationale: Trajectories include refunds to gift cards (task 112) as well as cards/PayPal (tasks 13, 104); confirmation guidance should explicitly cover gift-card refunds and provide identifying info for them.

all_deltas.content: IF performing an exchange or modification that changes price -> compute price_difference = new_price - old_price and include payment_method_id (the chosen payment method from the user's account) in the tool call. By default use the order's original payment method; if the user explicitly requests a different payment method that is already on their account, use that method but obtain explicit user confirmation before proceeding. If price_difference < 0, issue refund to the chosen payment method; if price_difference > 0, charge the chosen payment method. Do not send refunds to payment methods not on the user's account.
all_deltas.rationale: Agents in the trajectories honored the customer's explicit choice to use a different payment method on the same account for exchanges/refunds rather than always using the original order payment method.

all_deltas.content: IF the user requests a refund to a different payment method than the original -> inform the user that refunds default to the original payment method but can be issued to another payment method on their account or to an existing gift card if they explicitly request and confirm; refunds cannot be sent to external payment methods not on the account. If no eligible on-account method or gift card exists, offer refund only to the original payment method, offer a gift card if available, or offer escalation to a human agent.
all_deltas.rationale: Trajectories show agents allowed refunds/charges to a different card on the customer's account after explicit confirmation; update messaging to reflect this allowed path while prohibiting off-account refunds.

all_deltas.content: IF user requests refund to a different payment method than the order's original AND the requested method is NOT on the user's account AND there is no existing gift card on account -> refuse the redirect and offer refund only to the original payment method or to an existing gift card. IF the requested method is on the user's account -> allow the redirect but require explicit user confirmation before proceeding.
all_deltas.rationale: Existing rule disallowed redirects unless a gift card existed; trajectories show redirects to other on-account payment methods were accepted after user confirmation, so the precondition should be refined to permit on-account redirects with confirmation while still blocking off-account redirects.

all_deltas.content: IF an agent cannot fulfill a user's request due to system limitations (inventory, payment constraints), policy restrictions, or upon explicit user request for human help -> call transfer_to_human_agents(summary) with a concise summary of the customer's issue and offer to transfer. Record that transfer was requested/completed and notify the user that they are being connected to a human agent.
all_deltas.rationale: Trajectories include an instance where the agent escalated/ transferred the customer to a human agent when unable to complete an exchange due to payment constraints; the playbook should define escalation behavior and the transfer_to_human_agents tool usage.

all_deltas.content: IF identity is verified AND order.status == 'pending' AND user requests a shipping address change -> call modify_pending_order_address(order_id, address1, address2, city, state, country, zip) THEN confirm the updated shipping address with the user and note any effects on shipping or total price (if applicable).
all_deltas.rationale: Trajectories show address changes on pending orders using modify_pending_order_address (conversations 3 and 5); the playbook has no rule for handling address updates.

all_deltas.content: IF the user asks to reuse the shipping address from a previous order or to use an address saved on a prior order -> call get_order_details(previous_order_id) to retrieve that order's shipping address, present the retrieved address to the user, and ask for confirmation before applying it to the current order.
all_deltas.rationale: Agents retrieved and reused an address from a prior order when the user requested it (conversation 3); the playbook lacks an explicit rule for this flow.

all_deltas.content: IF a user requests a faster-than-standard refund or processing timeframe (e.g., asks to guarantee processing within fewer days than policy) -> inform the user of the standard timeframe, state that the agent cannot guarantee a faster processing time, and offer to escalate the request to a human agent who may be able to assist with expediting.
all_deltas.rationale: A user requested a 3-day refund window and the agent could not guarantee it but offered to escalate to a human agent (conversation 1); the playbook should explicitly capture this standard response.

all_deltas.content: IF identity is verified AND order.status == 'pending' AND user requests cancellation -> ask the user for a cancellation reason (provide common options if helpful), ask the user to explicitly confirm the cancellation, THEN call cancel_pending_order(order_id, reason). After completion, inform the user of the refund amount, the refund destination payment method (including identifying info), and the expected timeframe.
all_deltas.rationale: Conversations show agents collecting a cancellation reason and requesting explicit confirmation before calling cancel_pending_order, and then summarizing refunds (conversation 4 and 5). acti-003 should require collecting a reason and explicit confirmation to match observed behavior.

all_deltas.content: IF user provides a user_id -> call get_user_details(user_id) to retrieve account information.
all_deltas.rationale: Trajectories show interactions where a user supplied an account user_id; the system should accept that and call get_user_details directly.

all_deltas.content: IF the user has not provided any of the following: email OR (first name, last name, zip) OR user_id -> do not perform account-specific actions until identity info is provided and get_user_details has been called.
all_deltas.rationale: Clarifies that providing a user_id is an acceptable identity signal; avoid blocking account-specific actions when a valid user identifier is supplied.

all_deltas.content: IF identity is verified AND order.status == 'pending' AND user requests an address update for that order -> call modify_pending_order_address(order_id, address fields) to update the order address, THEN call modify_user_address(user_id, address fields) to update the account address (if the user requests), and return updated order/user details.
all_deltas.rationale: One trajectory required updating both the pending order address and the account address; the playbook had no explicit rule for address updates.

all_deltas.content: IF performing an exchange or modification that changes price -> compute price_difference = new_price - old_price. Ask the user which payment method they want to use for any additional charge (if price_difference > 0). Include payment_method_id (the chosen payment method) in the tool call for charging the difference. If price_difference < 0, issue the refund to the original order payment method (or to an existing gift card on the account if the user prefers and one exists). Clearly inform the user of the refund amount, the destination payment method, and the expected timeframe.
all_deltas.rationale: Trajectories show customers choosing a different payment method to pay extra (e.g., PayPal or gift card) while refunds consistently go to the original method or an account gift card; the rule should allow a user-chosen payment method for charges and enforce refund destination rules.

all_deltas.content: IF executing an action via a tool call (exchange/return/modify/cancel) -> ensure the tool call includes order_id, affected item_ids, the chosen new_item_ids (for exchange/modify), and payment_method_id (the payment method chosen for any additional charge). For refunds (price_difference < 0 or return), ensure the refund destination is the original order payment method or an existing gift card per refund policy.
all_deltas.rationale: Tool calls in the trajectories included payment_method_id chosen by the user when paying price differences; the rule should require inclusion of the chosen payment method for charges and explicitly enforce refund destination constraints.

all_deltas.content: IF identity is verified AND order.status == 'pending' AND the user requests a shipping address change -> call modify_pending_order_address(order_id, address1, address2, city, state, country, zip) to update the order's shipping address; ensure the tool call includes the order_id and the full new address and confirm the updated address with the user after the change.
all_deltas.rationale: Trajectories show agents successfully updating the shipping address for a pending order via modify_pending_order_address; the playbook lacks a rule covering address changes on pending orders.

all_deltas.content: IF order.status == 'pending' -> allowed actions: item modifications, address modifications, or cancellation (use modify_pending_order_items, modify_pending_order_address, or cancel_pending_order); exchanges/returns for delivered items are not applicable.
all_deltas.rationale: Evidence that pending orders can have their shipping address modified (not just item modifications or cancellations) requires expanding the allowed pending-order actions.

all_deltas.content: IF a user requests expedited processing or a faster-than-standard refund timeline -> inform the user of the standard timeframe for refunds/processing, state that you cannot guarantee an earlier processing time, and offer to escalate the request to a human agent or support team for further assistance.

all_deltas.rationale: Trajectories show a tool call modify_pending_order_address used to change shipping for a pending order (conversation 22). The playbook does not currently specify this action.

all_deltas.content: IF the user updates their account/default address -> do NOT automatically change existing orders' shipping addresses. Orders must be updated explicitly. Additionally, IF order.status == 'processed' (or otherwise already fulfilled) -> do not allow shipping address changes for that order; inform the user the address cannot be changed for processed/fulfilled orders and offer alternatives (e.g., contact support or update future orders).
all_deltas.rationale: In conversation 22, processed orders could not have their addresses changed while the pending order was updated separately; changing the account address did not retroactively change the pending order after the user changed the account back. Playbook needs a rule preventing automatic propagation and forbidding changes to processed orders.

all_deltas.content: IF a user requests an account/default address update -> explicitly ask whether they want that address applied to any existing orders, and if so, ask which specific order IDs to update. If the user does not explicitly request order updates, assume existing orders remain unchanged.

Do not process refunds to alternative payment methods without prior authorization or an available gift card.
